topic: Align Bagakit with the verified harness-devloop user-demand loop requirements
objective: Execute the first stable Bagakit slice: lock the target boundary, define a planning-entry handoff contract, and record the execution path in one machine-readable checklist.

items.title: Record task-local selector preflight for this non-trivial Bagakit-shaped change
verification: Selector preflight is recorded with a non-pending answer and one selected skill plan.

items.title: Freeze the Bagakit-side target boundary in a migration note
verification: The note keeps Bagakit clean-room boundaries and rejects bundled runtime expansion as the immediate next step.

items.title: Define the first stable planning-entry handoff contract
verification: The contract defines a stable payload shape, trust rule, and downstream materialization gate.
The contract keeps brainstorm, feature-tracker, flow-runner, and outer-driver ownership separate.

items.title: Index the new migration and spec artifacts in their owning surfaces
verification: The new artifacts are discoverable from the owning surface README files.

items.title: Capture the execution plan itself as one machine-readable JSON checklist
verification: The checklist is valid JSON and records done versus pending phases with explicit evidence references.

items.title: Materialize one explicit brainstorm to feature-tracker bridge above the approved handoff contract
verification: One approved planning-entry handoff can initialize or update canonical tracker truth without scraping brainstorm prose.

items.title: Tighten the explicit feature-tracker to flow-runner activation contract
verification: Execution-ready activation is explicit and replayable once planning truth is canonical.

items.title: Add one end-to-end planning-entry regression for the full route
verification: One deterministic or minimally mocked regression proves the route from clarification to execution-ready state.

next_action: Promote the stabilized semantics into the next canonical architecture or stewardship surface when the repo wants to elevate this migration result.
